summary: 5/5 acceptance criteria pass. The critical registry entry bug from the first test round is fixed — shutdown(keepServe=true) now preserves the registry entry so cleanupStaleServes can discover the healthy serve on the next daemon startup. Verified with targeted reproduction script and full test suite (1067 pass, 0 fail).

criteriaResults.criterion: POST /restart triggers graceful shutdown with keepServe=true
criteriaResults.evidence: Code: server.ts:2073-2078, index.ts:619-622, index.ts:535-539. Unit test confirms.

criteriaResults.criterion: Serve process continues running after daemon restart
criteriaResults.evidence: Fix at index.ts:529-533 wraps removeLegionEntryFn in if (!keepServe). Reproduction script confirms cleanupStaleServes returns preserved serve info.

criteriaResults.criterion: New daemon reconnects to existing serve
criteriaResults.evidence: cleanupStaleServes finds dead daemon + healthy serve, returns PID/port. adoptServe called at index.ts:377-381. Port reused via preservedServePort.

criteriaResults.criterion: legion restart CLI command with fallback
criteriaResults.evidence: cli/index.ts:455-500. Sends POST /restart, waits for PID exit, starts new daemon. Fallback to stop+start.
bun test: 1067 pass, 0 fail. Lint clean, typecheck clean. CI green.

documentationFeedback: AGENTS.md and daemon AGENTS.md correctly document the new command and endpoint. PR description is clear and comprehensive.
observations: Fix is minimal: single if (!keepServe) guard around removeLegionEntryFn
cleanupStaleServes removes stale entry after extracting serve info — no orphaned entries
Test added expect(removeLegionEntryCalls).toHaveLength(0) to directly cover the bug
